India A Collapse as Bangladesh A Win Thriller in Super Over

Nov 24, 2025

India A, despite a promising start, suffered a heartbreaking defeat to Bangladesh A in a tense Super Over during the Asia Cup Rising Stars semifinal in Doha. The dramatic exit came after India A allowed the match to slip away in the final overs and faltered under pressure during the Super Over, highlighting the fine margins of competitive cricket.

India A Struggle in Crucial Moments Against Bangladesh A

The India A side entered the semifinal with confidence but could not maintain consistency to overcome Bangladesh A. Their chase matched Bangladesh’s 194-run total, sending the contest into a Super Over, where India A made questionable tactical choices. Their inability to capitalize on strong individual innings exposed temperament issues under pressure.

Bangladesh A’s Total Sets High Bar

Bangladesh A posted 194/6 in 20 overs. Habibur Rahman’s composed 65 off 46 balls laid the foundation, while Meherob Hasan’s late assault, including four sixes and a four, turned the innings and exposed India A’s lack of control in the final overs.

India A’s Expensive Final Overs

Part-time bowler Naman Dhir conceded 28 runs in the 19th over. Meherob and Yasir Ali added 48 runs in the last two overs, which shifted momentum entirely in favor of Bangladesh A.

India A Chase Starts with Firepower from Suryavanshi and Arya

India A’s chase began strongly, as Vaibhav Suryavanshi and Priyansh Arya smashed 53 runs in 3.3 overs, putting early pressure on Bangladesh A.

Suryavanshi’s Explosive Start

Suryavanshi scored 38 off 15 balls, attacking Ripon Mondol and Meherob Hasan with back-to-back sixes. His dismissal due to a grip error triggered a shift in momentum.

Arya Extends India A’s Dominance Before Falling

Arya contributed 44 off 23 balls but was dismissed at deep mid-wicket, leading to a series of middle-order collapses.

Middle-Order Effort Keeps India A in the Contest

Captain Jitesh Sharma and Nehal Wadhera added 52 runs for the fourth wicket, reviving hopes as India A reached 150/3 in the 15th over.

Jitesh and Wadhera Push Toward the Target

Jitesh scored 33 off 23 balls, Wadhera remained unbeaten on 32. Jitesh’s dismissal via sharp stumping disrupted the chase, and the middle order failed to accelerate in the final overs.

India A Lose Momentum in Final Overs

Despite big hitters like Ramandeep Singh and Ashutosh Sharma, India A scored only 44 runs in the last 30 balls. They finished with a dramatic tie at 194/6, forcing a Super Over.

Super Over Disaster Seals India A’s Elimination

India A made questionable choices in the Super Over. In-form hitters Suryavanshi and Arya were not sent to bat; instead, Jitesh, Ashutosh, and Ramandeep faced the challenge.

Bangladesh A Hold Nerve Under Pressure

Ripon Mondol bowled a flawless Super Over, dismissing Jitesh and Ashutosh for ducks. India A ended the over without scoring.

Bangladesh A Complete the Job

Bangladesh A lost Yasir Ali on the first ball, but a wide from Suyash Sharma gifted the winning run, sealing India A’s exit.

India A Exit After Costly Errors and Lost Opportunities

The semifinal loss left India A with lessons to learn. Strong starts, competitive chases, and individual brilliance were overshadowed by poor tactical decisions and critical lapses under pressure.

Bangladesh A March Confidently Toward the Final

Bangladesh A advance to the final, awaiting the winner of Pakistan Shaheens vs Sri Lanka A. Their composure in key moments positions them well for the tournament’s biggest stage.
